name: State of Michigan
summary: Michigan sets the laws, standards, and statewide offices that every town in the state works within.

bodies.name: Office of the Governor
bodies.cadence: Full-time office; the Governor and Lieutenant Governor serve four-year terms.
bodies.role: Runs the state's executive branch, signs or vetoes bills, and proposes the state budget.
bodies.note: The Governor and Lieutenant Governor run together on one ticket and are elected statewide.

bodies.name: Office of the Attorney General
bodies.role: The state's top lawyer; represents Michigan in court and enforces state law.

bodies.name: Office of the Secretary of State
bodies.role: Runs elections statewide and the offices where residents handle licenses and vehicle titles.

bodies.name: Michigan Legislature
bodies.cadence: Convenes year-round in Lansing, with most work in regular session.
bodies.role: Writes and passes state law. It has two chambers: a 38-seat Senate and a 110-seat House of Representatives.
bodies.note: Each town falls within one state Senate district and one state House district.

officials.name: Garlin Gilchrist II
officials.note: Runs on the same ticket as the Governor.

benchmarks.label: Lead action level
benchmarks.note: This is the federal level at which a water system must act. Michigan's own rule is stricter and lowers the action level to 12 ppb, starting in 2025. A town's water report can be read against these numbers.

source.label: U.S. EPA, Lead and Copper Rule
